pairs.question: Which teams played in the 2016 World Series, and what was the outcome?
pairs.answer: The 2016 World Series was played between the Chicago Cubs (National League champions) and the Cleveland Indians (American League champions). The Chicago Cubs defeated the Cleveland Indians 4–3 to win their first World Series championship since 1908.

pairs.question: Why did the Cleveland Indians have home-field advantage in the 2016 World Series?
pairs.answer: The Cleveland Indians had home-field advantage in the 2016 World Series because the American League won the 2016 All-Star Game. However, this was the last World Series where home-field advantage was determined by the All-Star Game result; since 2017, it has been awarded to the team with the better regular-season record.

pairs.question: Describe the decisive Game 7 of the 2016 World Series.
pairs.answer: Game 7 of the 2016 World Series was won by the Chicago Cubs 8–7 in ten innings against the Cleveland Indians. It was the fifth World Series Game 7 to go into extra innings, the first since 1997 (which the Indians also lost), and the first to include a rain delay during the 10th inning.

pairs.question: How did the Chicago Cubs end their World Series championship drought in 2016?
pairs.answer: The Chicago Cubs ended their 108-year World Series championship drought by defeating the Cleveland Indians 4–3 in the 2016 World Series. This was their third championship overall and first since 1908, marking the end of the longest championship drought in North American professional sports history.

pairs.question: What historical significance did the 2016 World Series hold for both franchises?
pairs.answer: The 2016 World Series was historically significant because the Chicago Cubs and Cleveland Indians entered the series as the two franchises with the longest World Series title droughts, a combined 176 seasons without a championship. The Cubs won their third title (first since 1908), while the Indians, who had not won since 1948, lost their sixth appearance in the Fall Classic.

pairs.question: Who was the manager of the Cleveland Indians during the 2016 World Series, and what was his prior World Series experience?
pairs.answer: The Cleveland Indians were managed by Terry Francona during the 2016 World Series. Prior to this, Francona had won World Series titles with the Boston Red Sox in 2004 and 2007. He fell short in his bid to become the third manager to win his first three trips to the World Series.

pairs.question: How many times have the Chicago Cubs and Cleveland Indians faced each other in the postseason prior to 2016?
pairs.answer: The 2016 World Series was the first postseason meeting between the Chicago Cubs and Cleveland Indians in franchise history. Their matchup marked the first time these two teams had faced each other in the playoffs.
